Yes — film everything you CAN. Not random footage, but **every time you have a real reaction, film it.** That habit alone will build your content library fast.

Here are your **first 5 videos** to shoot this week. Simple, low-production, built for your energy:

---

## 🎬 Video 1 — "This is Yummy or Disgusting" (Series Opener)
**Shoot:** 5 different foods. For each one — look at camera, say "yummy or disgusting?" then try it and give your verdict with a reaction.
**Why:** This is your series anchor. Binary format = comments section gold. People will argue in the comments for you.
**Hook line:** *"I'm trying 5 foods. Yummy or disgusting? Let's find out."*

---

## 🎬 Video 2 — I Tried The [CURRENT TREND] So You Don't Have To
**Shoot:** Find one food trend (check TikTok's Explore page right now) and try it with your honest reaction.
**Why:** Trending audio + your opinion = algorithmic boost. The algorithm already knows what's hot, you're just giving it your twist.
**Hook line:** *"Everyone's talking about [trend]. I had to see if it was actually good."*

---

## 🎬 Video 3 — Soft Serve vs. Hard Ice Cream (Full Review)
**Shoot:** Go to a place that has soft serve, get some, compare it to hard ice cream side by side. Same verdict as before but extended, funnier, more chaotic.
**Why:** This is the video that already went viral for you. Do it again but BETTER. The algorithm already knows this content works.
**Hook line:** *"Everyone asked me about this. Here it is one more time."*

---

## 🎬 Video 4 — Eating My Own Song (Reaction + Food Combo)
**Shoot:** Play one of your songs in the background while eating food. React to your own music in real time — "wait this part hits different" or "this beat is crazy over a burger."
**Why:** Dual content. You're promoting your music AND food at the same time. TikTok rewards original audio. Plus uniqueness = saves.
**Hook line:** *"I made this song. Now I'm eating to it. Judge both."*

---

## 🎬 Video 5 — Quick Food Review: [Any restaurant or takeout]
**Shoot:** One location, one meal, under 90 seconds. No editing needed — just raw POV filming while you eat and talk.
**Why:** This is your bread and butter content. Max The Meat Guy built 3M doing almost exactly this. Relatable, easy to batch film.
**Hook line:** *"Grabbed [food]. Here's my real take on it."*

---

**Quick batch filming tip:** Do videos 1 and 5 back to back in one sitting. Same energy, easy to film 3-4 at once.

Which one are you most excited to shoot first? And what food do you have access to this week — restaurants, takeout, stuff at home? That'll help me pick the best one to lead with.
</final>
